title: Modular Building Block Construction with Save/Load
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js (via CDN) that implements a modular building block construction system with full save/load state management.

## Scene Setup
- Use a perspective camera at position (8, 10, 12) looking at origin.
- Add a ground plane (30x30, color #8B8878) at y=0 with grid helper (30 divisions, color #555555).
- Add ambient light (intensity 0.5) and a directional light at (5, 15, 8) with intensity 0.8 and castShadow enabled.
- Enable renderer shadows (PCFSoftShadowMap). Background color #1a1a2e.
- Add OrbitControls for camera rotation.

## Block System
- Blocks are unit cubes (1x1x1). They snap to an integer grid so every block center has integer x, y, z coordinates.
- Blocks stack on top of each other; y coordinate starts at 0.5 for ground level (center of first block).
- Provide 6 block colors selectable via keyboard: R=Red(#e74c3c), G=Green(#2ecc71), B=Blue(#3498db), Y=Yellow(#f1c40f), P=Purple(#9b59b6), O=Orange(#e67e22). Default selected color is Red.
- A translucent ghost block (opacity 0.4) follows the mouse cursor on the grid, snapping to valid placement positions. The ghost block shows where the next block will be placed.
- The ghost block must account for stacking: if hovering over an existing block, the ghost appears one unit above it.

## Placement & Removal
- LEFT CLICK places a block at the ghost position. Each block gets a unique incremental integer ID starting from 1.
- RIGHT CLICK (with contextmenu prevented) on an existing block removes it. Only the topmost block in any column can be removed.
- Blocks cannot be placed outside the grid bounds: x in [-7, 7], z in [-7, 7], y (center) in [0.5, 10.5] (max 10 blocks high).

## Undo/Redo System
- Press Ctrl+Z to undo the last action (place or remove). Undone actions go onto a redo stack.
- Press Ctrl+Y to redo. The redo stack clears whenever a new action is performed (not via redo).
- Each history entry stores: {type: 'place'|'remove', blockData: {id, x, y, z, color}}.

## Save/Load System
- Add a DOM panel (top-right, z-index 100) with:
  - 'Save' button (id='btn-save'): serializes current blocks into a JSON string stored in window.__3D_STATE__.savedData and also in localStorage key 'P1107_save'.
  - 'Load' button (id='btn-load'): restores blocks from window.__3D_STATE__.savedData (or localStorage). Clears undo/redo history after load.
  - 'Clear All' button (id='btn-clear'): removes all blocks, pushes a single undo entry of type 'clear' that stores all removed blocks.
  - 'Block Count' display (id='block-count'): shows current number of placed blocks.
  - 'Current Color' display (id='current-color'): shows name of selected color.
  - Save slot indicator (id='save-status'): shows 'Empty' or 'Saved (N blocks)' based on savedData.

## State Management (window.__3D_STATE__)
Expose the following on window.__3D_STATE__:
- blocks: Array of {id: number, x: number, y: number, z: number, color: string} for all currently placed blocks.
- blockCount: integer count of placed blocks.
- selectedColor: string name of current color ('Red','Green','Blue','Yellow','Purple','Orange').
- ghostPosition: {x, y, z} or null if ghost not visible.
- undoStackSize: integer length of undo stack.
- redoStackSize: integer length of redo stack.
- nextBlockId: the next ID that will be assigned.
- savedData: string|null, the last saved JSON string.
- lastAction: {type: string, blockId: number|null} describing the most recent action performed.
- maxHeight: number, the current maximum y-center among all placed blocks (0 if none).
- columnCount: number of unique (x,z) columns that have at least one block.
- totalVolume: number equal to blockCount (each block is 1 cubic unit).
- gridBounds: {minX: -7, maxX: 7, minZ: -7, maxZ: 7, maxY: 10.5}.

Update window.__3D_STATE__ synchronously after every action. Ensure all numerical values are never NaN or Infinity.
physics_constraints: Blocks must snap to integer grid positions. Blocks stack with no floating blocks allowed — a block at y=1.5 requires a block at y=0.5 in the same (x,z) column. Only topmost blocks in a column can be removed. Maximum stack height is 10 (y center = 10.5). totalVolume must always equal blockCount. columnCount must equal the number of distinct (x,z) pairs with blocks. Conservation: after undo of a place, blockCount decreases by exactly 1; after undo of a remove, blockCount increases by exactly 1.
